text: The submitters, the co-sponsors of Document MEPC, ES22 for their document, I'd like to committee to know that there was broad support in general for the establishment of a G.S.U.P. committee and exploring the organization's G.S.U. Working arrangements to effectively organize overall G.S.U. workload. The committee also need to know that several delegations expressed the need to further consider possible budgetary implications, including the availability of forms to support participation of all delegations, needing frequency and terms of reference. I believe that the discussion has been very useful and based on this discussion, we can request the secretary to provide additional information on possible working arrangements. There are attempts of reference to continue to account the work plan and means to ensure participation of all delegations to the next section. We can also invite counsel to further consider necessary budget arrangements for the next Biannian for a possible G.S.U.P. committee. We'll be understanding that a final decision on the establishment will be taking at a future session of the committee. That will be my submission and proposal to the committee. Okay. We'll see the recording. EJEP, please. Thank you, Chair. And thank you, SGE, for replying on questions raised during the discussions. Actually, I do agree that the budgetary and financial implications would take further discussions among council members. However, I have a question and I have a proposal. First, regarding and the replies on the questions, the SGE mentioned there would be need for additional stuff for MED if a new subcommittee is established. My question is to understand more that in case a new subcommittee is not established, then the secretary doesn't need any additional stuff. That's first. Secondly, I need to have a comparison between the cost of an ISWG as well as the subcommittee. I need to understand the difference financially. Secondly, my proposal would be MEPC agree in principle on the establishment of the subcommittee. We send it to the council regarding the financial and work plan arrangements. We can adjust the timing of the first session in accordance to what we can see best for the organization. So, in that case, we do have the option not to delay it for a full biennium. However, we still have the option to launch the subcommittee whenever we see it best. Thank you, Chair. Thank you for those questions and proposal. SD, you want to take his hand? Sam de la. The costs of an inter-sessional work in groups are already included in the budget organization, but excludes matters related to interpretation, the hybrid system, for interpretation and so on. And of course, no translation of documents, so that reduces the costs. As to a subcommittee, well, let me make this clear. It's your decision to decide about the subcommittee. We will follow your decisions. But there would be some matters that have to be taken into account before we can respond as to exactly when we could have these meetings agreed. The cost of the inter-sessional working group does not include what a subcommittee does. If the subcommittee works like others and with the normal rules of procedure of the committee, they can establish working groups and drafting groups. And for that, we need additional staff in the division. The division is also working at capacity. I do this harp on about this. We're always being asked to undertake additional work. And before we draw up the terms of reference for new subcommittee. The response is not always positive when I ask for additional staff to comply with all the requests. In the document, it says that at least initially we would need administrative assistance and the cost is around 120,000 pounds per annum. But as the work expands, then in order for us to respond both within conference division and in the MED, at some point, we will have to come to you to ask for additional staff. And as to when a meeting would take place, we don't just look at a single subcommittee. We have to look at the entire work program for all the subcommittees and the existing outputs when they have to report the committees, the deadlines for completion, when we have to report on amendments, and that is part of our planning process, which takes virtually a year before we can then submit it to you. And also avoid clashes in weeks when we've been told that we shouldn't have meetings because of decisions that have already been taken by the organisation. And that is why we have to try to weigh up and balance where we can accommodate meetings, which meetings have to be shifted, what impact that might have on the working plan or work plan that's been agreed by Member States. Thank you. Thank you Secretary General. EJ before coming back to the issue of the committee, I agree in principle at this time. I will come back to that, but first I'd like to take the floor of Mashra Islands, you know, our emerald and Saudi Arabia. Mashra Islands, please. Thank you, thank you very much Mr. Chair, and also many thanks to Secretary General. I feel you're paying. Not paying. We just wanted to make sure you mentioned about the terms of reference. We're looking forward to a very thorough discussion further on down the line with regards to the economics resources, etc. But ourselves and the Cook Islands did mention about MEPC and MSC and the work streams that are growing at MSC. And we would like to just highlight that we would like to see something in the terms of reference provisionals for the new subcommittee reform, please. I know MSC will be able to report or use subcommittee, but in our minds it's different. We would like them to be a joint. Thank you very much. Yes, Mashra Islands. We did an MEPC on reference on our special regard, so we should request the Secretary to provide additional information on possible working arrangements and of course draft terms of reference. So with your comments here, I'm planning to show the Secretary will consider those comments. And if that subcommittee will be and should be working with both MSC and MEPC, without taking the floor of our emerald, please. Thank you, Mr. Chair. At the outset, we would like to express our condolences to Kenya because of the passing of the former Kenyan Prime Minister Raela Odinga. We would like to thank you for your summary, Mr. Chair, but after listening to the different delegations, you can see that there is a wide support for the establishment of a new subcommittee and some issues that were raised could be discussed in that working group during this session, especially the five or eight meeting and also the terms of reference. And based on this, we can make a decision. It is possible in principle that the new subcommittee could be held the meetings of that subcommittee could be held in 2027. However, this will depend on the discussions during the working group meetings. Therefore, we see that it is best to refer this matter to the working group and then look at the recommendations of the working group. Thank you, Mr. Chair. Saudi Arabia, please. Thank you very much, Chair. We've been listening very closely to the room and explanations. It first allows us to thank you, Chair, and the Secretary General for the thorough information presented. It's been useful to make sure that our approach to this is informed. We did hear from several delegations that in principle, they don't see an issue with establishing the subcommittee. I do think that the conversation is very nuanced. Perhaps needs to be discussed in more detail in a different setting. So we would support what the United Emirates had put forward as a way that we can deal with this issue, which is to send this discussion to the working group and allow for more detailed, thorough discussion on the establishment of the subcommittee and to allow the working group to send back a recommendation to the committee on this matter. Another issue that we wanted to raise is we fully understand the points and the valid points and information raised by the Secretary General. We think in that regard, particularly in given the busy schedule that's upcoming for IMO meetings, in addition to the list of elements, potentially, Chair, that you listed. If we were to take that approach, we would also recommend including information from the Secretaryate on ongoing efforts taken to enhance efficiency of the organization and recommendations on how to improve efficiency gains moving forward. This information could be helpful in this context given we're talking about balancing between the need for work and the availability of resources. And we believe efficiency is a key part of this conversation. So we would have to, of course, have an informed discussion on this. And so paper that explains the ongoing efforts to increase the efficiency, as well as recommendations with rationales on ways that efficiency can be enhanced could be useful. Allow me also to take this opportunity to express our full support for the IMO Secretaryate and our continuous will to ensure that the IMO is well-resourced and has enough resources and capacity to conduct its work in an effective manner. And our careful attention to ensure the organization is enabled to the fullest extent. Thank you very much. Thank you, Saudi Arabia. Our non-gayfully flow to the Secretary General to talk about the issue of efficiency. Thank you, Secretary General. Thank you for the discussion. I'd like to thank the distinguished delegate of Saudi Arabia for the question. From the beginning of last year, I have sought to find and implement all possible efficiencies within the organization. It's all been documented and reported to Council. And the documents are available. And the final budget report, which will be submitted in December this year also includes all the efficiencies. But including that, then I cannot, I am at capacity. I can't find any more efficiencies. And at some point, as I have already said, I can't do any more and then I have to request financial support. But you can find all the information in the documents that were submitted to Council both last year and this year. Thank you. Thank you, Secretary General. Grateful for you allowing me to come in again. I'm sorry, I'll tell the Secretary how to suck out, but it will reflect on the procedure with the CCC and the PPR relative to the relationship between the MEPC and the MSC with subcommittees that transcend both disciplines. I commend that to you. The PPR is interesting, of course, because as I try to remind the extraordinary session here today, this discussion and the way it has taken place relative to PPR, pollution prevention response, on the greenhouse gas issue, when the decision was made at that time, that it's such a political issue that it had to stay directly under the umbrella of the committee. That is why we ended up committing an intersexual working group under the committee. Anyway, we've lost that one. Of course, it does not mean that, if unfortunately, this subcommittee was established, that it would, in any way, take over the role and the mandate and the competence of the MSC on matters of safety-related matters. It would not do that. What, of course, would happen is that the safety-related matters and rest assured colleagues, there are going to be many as we pursue these alternative fuels that would then have to be through the MSC and then through its subcommittees. What you're putting in motion is going to be pretty complex, and I'm really not sure it's going to be for the benefit. I reiterate that we believe it would have been better to wait to strengthen the country and to force, keep it as we're going now, and then consider what the benefit, what the major political issues are out of the way of whether they would be a benefit and a need for a standalone subcommittee. Thank you. Thank you, Corpaglins, or Aqua, please. Thank you, Chair. First, I want to convey our condolences to our colleague from the Republic of Kenya. We thank the Secretary-General for all clarifications. We can observe the support that this proposal has got so far. So, here we align ourselves with Egypt and UAE and say that this timing is crucial. Establishing this subcommittees not merely an expense, but a crucial strategic investment to effectively tackle the MS technical workload required by our DHG strategy. Only dedicated and permanent body will provide the focused expertise and stability needed to deliver on our climate commitments and ensure the shipping industries transition to a zero-emission future. Thank you, Chair. Thank you, Morocco. Lebia, please. Thank you, Mr. Chair. Thank you very much. I would like to thank the Secretary-General for the explanations presented, especially with regard to the measures taken. Also, we support the proposal of the UAE with regard to referring this issue to the working group. This may give the chance to more discussions to be held in a concentrated manner. We do trust the Secretariat. We trust that it can deal with these challenges that have to do with the cost. It can implement this proposal. We trust that it will support us and give us enough information about dealing with this issue. When we discuss the current means, how possible or feasible is it to implement this proposal using the current means as to employing more staff or adding to the budget? This is something that we can discuss later when we decide whether we are going to go ahead. This is something that needs more information from the Secretariat. Thank you.
